news_summary: Today's technology news highlights groundbreaking advancements in supercomputing and semiconductor design, with China’s LineShine leading global rankings and IBM achieving record transistor density through innovative chip architectures.
news_summary_fr: L'actualité technologique d'aujourd'hui met en avant des avancées révolutionnaires dans le domaine du calcul intensif et de la conception de semi-conducteurs : le projet chinois LineShine occupe la tête des classements mondiaux, tandis qu'IBM atteint une densité de transistors record grâce à des architectures de puces innovantes.
news_summary_es: Las noticias tecnológicas de hoy destacan avances revolucionarios en supercomputación y diseño de semiconductores, con el proyecto chino LineShine a la cabeza de las clasificaciones mundiales y IBM alcanzando una densidad de transistores récord gracias a innovadoras arquitecturas de chips.

articles.title: China’s LineShine just topped the global supercomputer ranking: what you need to know
articles.summary: Nature, Published online: 25 June 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02047-wThe supercomputer has an innovative chip design that allows it to carry out more than 2 quintillion calculations per second.
articles.content:
LineShine is based at the National Supercomputing Center in Shenzhen (pictured), a hub for science and technology.Credit: Grandpa Cat/Alamy

A Chinese supercomputer has taken out first place on the list of the world’s fastest computers — the first China-based system to achieve this ranking in almost a decade. LineShine stands apart because it combines conventional computer processing with artificial-intelligence capabilities and represents the future of scientific computing, say researchers.

On Tuesday, LineShine, based at the National Supercomputing Center in Shenzhen, China, was placed first in the TOP500, which is published twice a year.

The computer is remarkable because it doesn’t use graphics processing units (GPUs), fast chips that can handle the concurrent calculations needed for AI computations. US companies have tended to lead advanced chip designs, but LineShine is made from Chinese parts, in part because the United States has restricted the export of its GPUs to China, citing national security concerns.

The machine’s power comes from combining sheer scale of components — made up of almost 14 million processing cores — with design innovations, including fast shuttling of data and specialized units that can perform some of the dense calculations that are performed by GPUs on other computers.

LineShine, which was publicly unveiled in April, can carry out more than 2 quintillion calculations per second, or 2.19 exaflops, making it 22% faster on the competition benchmark than its closest rival and previous top dog El Capitan at the Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory in California, which placed second.

Performance is judged on the TOP500 ranking’s ‘Linpack’ metric — a standard industry benchmark that measures a computer’s speed at solving a collection of linear equations.

Topping the ranking doesn’t necessarily make LineShine the fastest computer in the world for every scientific or AI application, says Jack Dongarra, a computer scientist at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville and co-creator of the TOP500 list. The huge computer systems that AI companies, such as Google and Meta, use today are also superfast, but are designed and built to perform less precise calculations at a higher rate, so are hard to compare. Plus, those firms often don’t participate in the TOP500 competition, says Dongarra.

Future of computing

But LineShine’s win is significant. The last time a Chinese computer topped the list was in 2017 with Sunway TaihauLight. The latest win demonstrates China’s “very substantial capabilities” in areas such as computer-processor design, says Dongarra, and it suggests ways that a single computer can support both conventional scientific computing and emerging AI computations. “That may be particularly valuable as scientific computing and AI become increasingly intertwined.”

Haohuan Fu, a computing and geoscience researcher at Tsinghua University in Beijing, China, who has used LineShine agrees that computers that bring these two capabilities together will be the next generation of scientific computing. “I am excited because systems like LineShine make it possible to study complex natural and engineered systems at larger scale, higher resolution and greater speed. More importantly, they allow us to bring together physical knowledge and data-driven AI in a much more integrated way,” he adds. Fu has used LineShine to show that conventional physics-based weather models can be combined with AI models to improve rainfall prediction models over East Asia.

Using the supercomputer, his team was able to test weather predictions between 2016 and 2025 in just 14.6 hours1. For real-world use, predictions must be done quickly enough for the forecast to be useful, he adds. “A typical computing cluster would not be able to support this combination of scale, speed and complexity.” LineShine has also been used by scientists to simulate magnetic materials at the atomic scale.

Homegrown technology
articles.reasoning: The article reports on a significant technological breakthrough where China's LineShine supercomputer, featuring innovative chip design and AI integration, topped the global supercomputer ranking. This achievement demonstrates substantial advancements in computing technology with broad implications for scientific research, AI development, and practical applications like improved weather prediction. The story is focused, detailed, and highlights a meaningful, large-scale impact on technology and science.
articles.personality_title: China’s LineShine becomes world’s fastest supercomputer with new chip design
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – Supercomputers are incredibly powerful machines used by scientists to solve complex problems quickly. The TOP500 list ranks the world’s fastest supercomputers twice a year.

**What happened** – On June 25, 2026, China’s LineShine supercomputer took first place on the TOP500 list. It is located at the National Supercomputing Center in Shenzhen and uses a new chip design made entirely from Chinese parts. LineShine can perform more than 2 quintillion calculations per second, making it 22% faster than the previous top computer, El Capitan in the US.

**Impact** – LineShine is special because it combines traditional computing with artificial intelligence (AI) capabilities without relying on the usual graphics chips (GPUs). This shows China’s strong progress in computer technology despite export restrictions on some US-made parts. The supercomputer helps researchers study complex natural systems, like improving weather forecasts by mixing physics and AI models. For example, it predicted rainfall over East Asia for ten years in just under 15 hours.

**What’s next step** – Scientists will continue using LineShine to explore new research areas, such as atomic-scale simulations and better AI models. This could lead to faster discoveries in science and technology, and inspire future supercomputers that blend AI and traditional computing even more effectively.

**One-sentence takeaway** – China’s LineShine supercomputer sets a new speed record using innovative homegrown chips, advancing both scientific computing and artificial intelligence.
articles.personality_title_fr: LineShine de Chine devient le superordinateur le plus rapide du monde grâce à une nouvelle puce
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Les superordinateurs sont des machines très puissantes utilisées par les scientifiques pour résoudre rapidement des problèmes complexes. Le classement TOP500 liste les superordinateurs les plus rapides du monde deux fois par an.

**Ce qui s’est passé** – Le 25 juin 2026, le superordinateur chinois LineShine a pris la première place du classement TOP500. Il est situé au Centre national de superinformatique de Shenzhen et utilise une nouvelle conception de puce entièrement fabriquée en Chine. LineShine peut effectuer plus de 2 quintillions de calculs par seconde, ce qui le rend 22 % plus rapide que l’ancien numéro un, El Capitan aux États-Unis.

**Impact** – LineShine est spécial car il combine l’informatique traditionnelle avec des capacités d’intelligence artificielle (IA) sans utiliser les puces graphiques habituelles (GPU). Cela montre les progrès importants de la Chine en technologie informatique malgré les restrictions sur certaines pièces américaines. Le superordinateur aide les chercheurs à étudier des systèmes naturels complexes, comme améliorer les prévisions météo en mêlant modèles physiques et IA. Par exemple, il a prédit les précipitations en Asie de l’Est sur dix ans en moins de 15 heures.

**Prochaine étape** – Les scientifiques continueront à utiliser LineShine pour explorer de nouveaux domaines de recherche, comme les simulations à l’échelle atomique et de meilleurs modèles d’IA. Cela pourrait accélérer les découvertes en science et technologie et inspirer des superordinateurs futurs qui combinent encore mieux IA et calcul traditionnel.

**En une phrase** – Le superordinateur chinois LineShine établit un nouveau record de vitesse avec des puces innovantes fabriquées localement, faisant progresser l’informatique scientifique et l’intelligence artificielle.
articles.personality_title_es: LineShine de China se convierte en la supercomputadora más rápida del mundo con nuevo diseño de chip
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – Las supercomputadoras son máquinas muy potentes que usan los científicos para resolver problemas complejos rápidamente. La lista TOP500 clasifica las supercomputadoras más rápidas del mundo dos veces al año.

**Qué pasó** – El 25 de junio de 2026, la supercomputadora china LineShine alcanzó el primer lugar en la lista TOP500. Está ubicada en el Centro Nacional de Supercomputación en Shenzhen y usa un nuevo diseño de chip hecho completamente en China. LineShine puede realizar más de 2 quintillones de cálculos por segundo, siendo un 22 % más rápida que la anterior número uno, El Capitan en Estados Unidos.

**Impacto** – LineShine es especial porque combina la computación tradicional con capacidades de inteligencia artificial (IA) sin usar las habituales unidades de procesamiento gráfico (GPU). Esto muestra el gran avance de China en tecnología informática a pesar de las restricciones en piezas estadounidenses. La supercomputadora ayuda a los investigadores a estudiar sistemas naturales complejos, como mejorar las predicciones del tiempo combinando modelos físicos y de IA. Por ejemplo, predijo las lluvias en Asia Oriental durante diez años en menos de 15 horas.

**Próximo paso** – Los científicos seguirán usando LineShine para explorar nuevas áreas de investigación, como simulaciones a escala atómica y mejores modelos de IA. Esto podría acelerar los descubrimientos en ciencia y tecnología e inspirar futuras supercomputadoras que integren mejor la IA y la computación tradicional.

**Resumen en una frase** – La supercomputadora china LineShine establece un nuevo récord de velocidad con chips innovadores de fabricación local, avanzando la computación científica y la inteligencia artificial.
articles.image_prompt: A detailed painting of a vast, intricate network of glowing golden circuits and interconnected processing cores shaped like a futuristic cityscape, symbolizing the LineShine supercomputer’s immense computational power and innovation, set against a soft, warm background of gentle reds and oranges evoking Shenzhen’s technological hub and China’s homegrown advancement in AI and scientific computing.

articles.title: Record-breaking IBM chip uses trick to cram in 100 billion transistors
articles.summary: IBM's latest chip packs in twice as many transistors as the current state-of-the-art chip by adding a second layer of silicon circuitry
articles.content:
IBM's latest chip packs in twice as many transistors as the current state-of-the-art chip by adding a second layer of silicon circuitry

IBM’s new chip IBM

There is a new record for tiny, powerful computer chips. IBM’s prototype chip is the size of a fingernail, yet packs in almost 100 billion transistors – nearly twice as many as the previous state-of-the-art chip – thanks to a three-dimensional technique.

The company says the 10 millimetre by 15 millimetre chip will offer 70 per cent higher energy efficiency and 50 per cent higher performance than current leading chips, and will be in commercial devices within 10 years.

For decades, the name given to chip-manufacturing processes referred to the size of individual transistors in nanometres: 10 nanometres, 5 nanometres, etc. And the smaller things got, the better – smaller components tend to mean higher density and therefore faster computation for a chip of a given size, as well as lower energy use.

But in recent years, the situation has become rather more complex, says Huiming Bu at IBM. Size names have now decoupled from any physical reality and have become nothing more than marketing terms.

So while IBM refers to this latest technology as “0.7 nanometre”, it doesn’t actually claim that its components are that small, even though it packs in transistors with record density. The real innovation – which Bu says took 15 years of development – is a process to stick together two layers of silicon chips that produces all the electrical connections needed between the two, doesn’t overheat and can be mass produced.

“Our whole industry has been scaling transistors in the X-axis dimension and the Y-axis dimension for all the 60-plus years [of chip manufacturing],” says Bu. “It’s the first time we’ll enable transistor scaling in the Z direction.”

IBM wouldn’t give precise details on the dimensions of components in the new technology, but the information they did release suggests that the technology is essentially two layers of the first working 2-nanometre chip, which IBM announced in 2021. That original technology is now manufactured by many of the world’s leading chip foundries and it is expected that it will make it into the next Apple iPhone.

Because of the vast complexity and cost involved in designing and making new chips, the industry collaborates on setting out a roadmap of what new technologies need to be created and brought to market and by when, orchestrated by the not-for-profit Interuniversity Microelectronics Centre, which does much of the research and development. Though IBM’s 0.7-nanometre technology has yet to be commercially tested, it is an expected step in global chip-making that will probably be followed by other makers.

Bu hopes that, in another decade, the new 0.7-nanometre chips will be making it to commercial devices, but says the technology is increasingly having to push against the laws of physics to battle unwanted quantum effects, current leakage and other problems that arise at such minuscule scales. Parts of the latest chips are just 15 silicon atoms thick.

Owen Guy at Swansea University in the UK says other chip makers are making claims about similarly high transistor density when they are actually using multiple layers of silicon separated by thick layers of substrate, which doesn’t allow true 3D design like IBM claims, as connections can’t be easily made from one layer to the other and can be problematic to cool. “There’s a lot of smoke and mirrors about this stuff now.”

He says chip components have gotten so small now that shrinking the size of transistors – even if a chip requires billions of them – isn’t going to make a device like a laptop or smartphone smaller in practice. The push for ever-smaller components is more about extracting better energy efficiency and cooling benefits to give mobile devices a better battery life or reduce the energy consumption of data centres.

One of the main challenges now will be how to integrate IBM’s new chip technology into the global manufacturing chain and get it into devices. Chips are made in batches of hundreds on a single 300-millimetre silicon wafer that contains trillions of individual transistors, then cut into individual parts. Complex machinery performs thousands of individual operations on the wafer, setting down layers of circuit, insulation and various chemicals with thicknesses of just a few nanometres. Adding an untested new feature like IBM’s second layer won’t be simple.

Some chip makers aspire to make an even smaller “0.2-nanometre” technology, at which point parts of the circuit could be a single atom wide. “The ultimate limit is one electron and one atom.” says Guy. “It’s probably around the 2050 mark where we’re going to have to have quantum technologies to make the next big leap.”
articles.reasoning: The article reports a significant technological breakthrough by IBM in chip manufacturing, achieving a record transistor density with a novel 3D layering technique. This innovation promises substantial improvements in energy efficiency and performance, with broad implications for consumer electronics and data centers globally. The story is focused, detailed, and highlights a major advancement with potential large-scale benefits.
articles.personality_title: IBM creates new chip with 100 billion transistors using a 3D design
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – For many years, computer chips have become faster and more powerful by making their tiny parts, called transistors, smaller and smaller. These transistors help the chip process information. But recently, making transistors smaller has become very hard because of physical limits.

**What happened** – IBM has built a new chip that fits almost 100 billion transistors into a small space about the size of a fingernail. They did this by stacking two layers of silicon circuits on top of each other, making the chip three-dimensional. This chip is called a “0.7 nanometre” chip, though that number is more of a name than an exact size. IBM’s new chip is expected to use 70% less energy and be 50% faster than today’s best chips.

**Impact** – This is the first time chip makers have successfully increased transistor density by building upwards (in the Z direction), not just by making parts smaller in length and width. This new 3D method could help devices like smartphones and laptops run faster and use less battery. It might also reduce energy use in big data centers, which use lots of power to run many chips.

**What’s next step** – IBM hopes this new chip technology will be used in real products within 10 years. However, it will be difficult to add this new design into current manufacturing processes because making chips involves many complex steps. Other companies might also try to use similar 3D designs in the future, but IBM’s method is unique because it connects the two layers well and keeps the chip cool.

**One-sentence takeaway** – IBM’s new chip uses a clever two-layer design to pack in almost 100 billion transistors, promising faster and more energy-efficient devices in the coming decade.
articles.personality_title_fr: IBM crée une nouvelle puce avec 100 milliards de transistors grâce à un design 3D
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Depuis des années, les puces informatiques deviennent plus rapides en réduisant la taille de leurs minuscules composants appelés transistors. Ces transistors permettent à la puce de traiter les informations. Mais il devient de plus en plus difficile de les rendre plus petits à cause des limites physiques.

**Ce qui s’est passé** – IBM a fabriqué une nouvelle puce qui contient presque 100 milliards de transistors dans un espace de la taille d’un ongle. Ils ont réussi cela en empilant deux couches de circuits en silicium, créant ainsi une puce en trois dimensions. Cette puce est appelée « 0,7 nanomètre », même si ce nombre est plus un nom qu’une taille exacte. La nouvelle puce d’IBM devrait utiliser 70 % d’énergie en moins et être 50 % plus rapide que les meilleures puces actuelles.

**Impact** – C’est la première fois que les fabricants augmentent la densité des transistors en construisant en hauteur (direction Z), et pas seulement en réduisant la longueur et la largeur. Cette nouvelle méthode 3D pourrait aider les smartphones et ordinateurs portables à être plus rapides et à consommer moins de batterie. Elle pourrait aussi réduire la consommation d’énergie dans les grands centres de données.

**Prochaine étape** – IBM espère que cette technologie sera utilisée dans des produits réels d’ici 10 ans. Cependant, il sera difficile d’intégrer ce nouveau design dans les méthodes de fabrication actuelles, car la production de puces est très complexe. D’autres entreprises pourraient aussi essayer d’utiliser des designs 3D similaires, mais la méthode d’IBM est unique car elle connecte bien les deux couches tout en gardant la puce fraîche.

**Résumé en une phrase** – La nouvelle puce d’IBM utilise un design à deux couches pour contenir près de 100 milliards de transistors, promettant des appareils plus rapides et économes en énergie dans la prochaine décennie.
articles.personality_title_es: IBM crea un nuevo chip con 100 mil millones de transistores usando un diseño 3D
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – Durante muchos años, los chips de computadora se han vuelto más rápidos haciendo sus partes diminutas, llamadas transistores, cada vez más pequeñas. Estos transistores ayudan al chip a procesar información. Pero recientemente, hacer los transistores más pequeños se ha vuelto muy difícil debido a límites físicos.

**Qué pasó** – IBM ha construido un nuevo chip que cabe casi 100 mil millones de transistores en un espacio del tamaño de una uña. Lo lograron apilando dos capas de circuitos de silicio una sobre otra, haciendo el chip tridimensional. Este chip se llama “0.7 nanómetros”, aunque ese número es más un nombre que un tamaño exacto. Se espera que el nuevo chip de IBM use un 70% menos de energía y sea un 50% más rápido que los mejores chips actuales.

**Impacto** – Esta es la primera vez que los fabricantes aumentan la densidad de transistores construyendo hacia arriba (en la dirección Z), no solo haciendo las partes más pequeñas en largo y ancho. Este nuevo método 3D podría ayudar a que dispositivos como teléfonos y laptops funcionen más rápido y usen menos batería. También podría reducir el uso de energía en grandes centros de datos.

**Próximo paso** – IBM espera que esta tecnología de chip se use en productos reales dentro de 10 años. Sin embargo, será difícil añadir este nuevo diseño a los procesos actuales de fabricación, porque hacer chips implica muchos pasos complejos. Otras compañías podrían intentar usar diseños 3D similares, pero el método de IBM es único porque conecta bien las dos capas y mantiene el chip fresco.

**Resumen en una frase** – El nuevo chip de IBM usa un diseño de dos capas para meter casi 100 mil millones de transistores, prometiendo dispositivos más rápidos y eficientes en energía en la próxima década.
articles.image_prompt: A detailed, warm-toned painting of a delicate, translucent silicon wafer layered like a glowing double-decker circuit board, with countless tiny, shimmering transistor-like lights densely packed in two stacked layers connected by fine, luminous threads, set against a soft, abstract background symbolizing advanced technology and innovation.

articles.title: IBM hails new 'block of flats' design breakthrough for ultra tiny chips
articles.summary: IBM says it has created the world's first known chip tech below 1 nanometre - but it will be some time before it's ready for production.
articles.content:
IBM has unveiled a new chip design which it says could enable manufacturers to cram 100 billion transistors on a silicon chip the size of a fingernail.

The current industry-standard size for chips, measured in a the unit of nanometres - a billionth of a metre and the size of a few atoms - is around two nanometres (nm).

But IBM claims its new chip tech is the equivalent of around 0.7nm, which may make it the world's first known chip technology below 1nm.

However, it will be several years before the chip tech could be ready to go into production.

The firm claims in tests, its prototype performed 50% better than its own 2nm chip and was 70% more energy efficient.

It claimed similar boosts in performance when it debuted its 2nm chip tech back in 2021 - saying at the time its tests of those, slightly larger, chips produced similar leaps in performance and energy efficiency.

Jay Gambetta, director of IBM Research and IBM Fellow, described the NanoStack tech as a "landmark moment" for the future of chips.

"With our new NanoStack architecture, we're not just making smaller transistors, we're reinventing how chips are built to deliver dramatically more power and energy efficiency," he said.
articles.reasoning: The article reports a significant technological breakthrough by IBM in chip design, achieving sub-1 nanometre transistor technology that promises major improvements in performance and energy efficiency. This advancement has broad implications for the semiconductor industry and future electronic devices, representing a meaningful step forward with clear potential benefits for society.
articles.personality_title: IBM unveils breakthrough chip design smaller than 1 nanometre
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – Computer chips contain tiny parts called transistors that help them work. The size of these parts is measured in nanometres (nm), which are billionths of a meter. Smaller transistors can make chips faster and use less energy. The current standard size is about 2nm.

**What happened** – IBM announced a new chip design called NanoStack that uses parts about 0.7nm in size, making it the first known chip technology smaller than 1nm. This design can fit 100 billion transistors on a chip the size of a fingernail. Tests showed the new chip performs 50% better and uses 70% less energy than IBM’s 2nm chips. However, it will take several years before this technology is ready for real use.

**Impact** – This breakthrough could change how chips are made by allowing more powerful and energy-saving devices. Smaller, more efficient chips can improve computers, phones, and other electronics, making them faster and longer-lasting. It also pushes the limits of how tiny technology can get.

**What's next step** – IBM will continue to develop and test the NanoStack technology before it can be produced widely. This process may take years as they work on manufacturing and making sure the chips are reliable.

**One-sentence takeaway** – IBM’s new NanoStack chip design is the first to go below 1 nanometre, promising faster and more energy-efficient electronics in the future.
articles.personality_title_fr: IBM dévoile une avancée dans les puces plus petites que 1 nanomètre
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Les puces informatiques contiennent de minuscules parties appelées transistors. Leur taille se mesure en nanomètres (nm), soit un milliardième de mètre. Des transistors plus petits rendent les puces plus rapides et moins gourmandes en énergie. La taille standard actuelle est d'environ 2 nm.

**Ce qui s'est passé** – IBM a annoncé un nouveau design de puce nommé NanoStack avec des parties de 0,7 nm, ce qui en fait la première technologie de puce connue sous 1 nm. Ce design peut contenir 100 milliards de transistors sur une puce de la taille d'un ongle. Les tests montrent que cette puce est 50 % plus performante et utilise 70 % moins d'énergie que les puces 2 nm d'IBM. Cependant, cette technologie ne sera pas prête avant plusieurs années.

**Impact** – Cette avancée pourrait changer la fabrication des puces en permettant des appareils plus puissants et économes en énergie. Des puces plus petites et efficaces peuvent améliorer les ordinateurs, téléphones et autres appareils électroniques, les rendant plus rapides et durables. Cela repousse aussi les limites de la miniaturisation.

**Prochaine étape** – IBM va continuer à développer et tester la technologie NanoStack avant une production à grande échelle. Ce processus prendra plusieurs années pour assurer la fiabilité et la fabrication.

**Conclusion en une phrase** – Le nouveau design NanoStack d’IBM est le premier à descendre sous 1 nanomètre, promettant des appareils électroniques plus rapides et économes en énergie à l’avenir.
articles.personality_title_es: IBM presenta un avance en chips más pequeños que 1 nanómetro
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – Los chips de computadora tienen partes muy pequeñas llamadas transistores. Su tamaño se mide en nanómetros (nm), que es una milmillonésima parte de un metro. Transistores más pequeños hacen que los chips sean más rápidos y usen menos energía. El tamaño estándar actual es de alrededor de 2 nm.

**Qué pasó** – IBM anunció un nuevo diseño de chip llamado NanoStack con partes de aproximadamente 0,7 nm, siendo la primera tecnología conocida de chip por debajo de 1 nm. Este diseño puede tener 100 mil millones de transistores en un chip del tamaño de una uña. Las pruebas mostraron que el nuevo chip funciona un 50 % mejor y usa un 70 % menos de energía que los chips de 2 nm de IBM. Sin embargo, tomará varios años antes de que esta tecnología esté lista para usarse.

**Impacto** – Este avance podría cambiar la forma en que se fabrican los chips, permitiendo dispositivos más potentes y que ahorran energía. Chips más pequeños y eficientes pueden mejorar computadoras, teléfonos y otros aparatos electrónicos, haciéndolos más rápidos y duraderos. También lleva la tecnología a tamaños más pequeños que nunca.

**Próximo paso** – IBM seguirá desarrollando y probando la tecnología NanoStack antes de que pueda producirse en masa. Este proceso puede tomar años para asegurar que los chips sean confiables.

**Resumen en una frase** – El nuevo diseño NanoStack de IBM es el primero en bajar de 1 nanómetro, prometiendo aparatos electrónicos más rápidos y eficientes en el futuro.
articles.image_prompt: A detailed, warm-toned painting of a tiny, intricately layered block of flats made of glowing, translucent silicon, stacked vertically like futuristic microchip architecture, surrounded by delicate, shimmering circuits that resemble a miniature cityscape glowing softly against a simple, natural background.
